Alarm Probable Cause: The alarm will occur while any sub-alarm occur in A-POI module.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check the  alarm source to find the root cause
2. Clear the related alarm to clear the overall alarm

Alarm Probable Cause: The detected channel power >51dBm.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Please check the RIU input power
2. Add the suitable attenuator on the input port of RIU

Alarm Probable Cause: The alarm will occur while any one of FANs fail.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check the connection of the FAN
2. Replace the related FAN module

Alarm Probable Cause: The alarm will occur while DC input power<38V or AC  input power<100V.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check the power socket connection
2. Change the PSU module for AC supply
3. Change the RIU

Alarm Probable Cause: The detected temperature greater than overtemperature threshold.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check if there is FAN alarm
2. Check the environment temperature, need to guarantee the environment temperature no more than 45 degree
3. Check the mechanical of the device, make sure there is no dust in the device
4. Keeps some clearance (20cm) of the device

Alarm Probable Cause: The detected channel power >49dBm(High Power)
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Please check the RIU input power
2. Add the suitable attenuator on the input port of RIU

Alarm Probable Cause: The detected channel power <17dBm(Low Power)
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Please check the input  power
2. Adjust the suitable attenuator on the input port of RIU
3. Check the RF input connection of RIU
4. Change the RIU

Alarm Probable Cause: The alarm will occur while any sub-alarm occur in the unit.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check the  alarm source to find the root cause
2. Clear the related alarm to clear the overall alarm

Alarm Probable Cause: The alarm will occur while DC input power<38V or AC  input power<100V.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check the input power voltage range
2. Check the connection of the power socket
3. Replace the module

Alarm Probable Cause: The alarm will occur while power supply in high temperature.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check if there is FAN alarm
2. Check the environment temperature, need to guarantee the environment temperature no more than 45 degree
3. Check the mechanical of the device, make sure there is no dust in the device
4. Keeps some clearance (20cm) of the device

Alarm Probable Cause: The alarm will occur while any one of FANs(totally 4) fail.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check the connection of the FAN
2. Replace the related FAN module

Alarm Probable Cause: The detected temperature greater than overtemperature threshold.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check if there is FAN alarm
2. Check the environment temperature, need to guarantee the environment temperature no more than 45 degree
3. Check the mechanical of the device, make sure there is no dust in the device
4. Keeps some clearance (20cm) of the device

Alarm Probable Cause:
Indicates one of the following SFP problems :TX/RX levels, Temperature,
TX/RX fault, TX/RX disable, absence (removing the SFP from the cage) .
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check if a fiber optic cable is disconnected, make sure the connection is stable
2. Check if SFP problem- try to replace with another SFP
3. Clean fiber optic cable/connector

Alarm Probable Cause:
Indicates one of the following SFP problems :TX/RX levels, Temperature,
TX/RX fault, TX/RX disable, absence (removing the SFP from the cage) .
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check if a fiber optic cable is disconnected, make sure the connection is stable
2. Check if SFP problem- try to replace with another SFP
3. Clean fiber optic cable/connector

Alarm Probable Cause: Indicates the SFP Synthesizer failure .
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check if a fiber optic cable is disconnected 
2. Check if SFP problem- try to replace with another SFP
3. Clean fiber optic cable/connector
4. Check if the pair of SFPs are in same type
5. Check the software version and its release note for the limitation

Alarm Probable Cause: Indicates the SFP link failure.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check if the fiber optic cable is disconnected 
2. Check if the Sync fault
2. Check if SFP problem - try to replace with another SFP
3. Clean fiber optic cable/connector
4. Check if the pair of SFPs are in same type
5. Reboot the device

Alarm Probable Cause: Indicates the SFP is in high temperature.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check if the device has the over temperature alarm, and deal with OverallAlarm 
2. Check the SFP install  
3. Change the SFP

Alarm Probable Cause: The alarm will occur while the hardware is fail
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check if any other alarms are occurred
2. Reboot the device
3. Change the device if hardware is fail

Alarm Probable Cause: Module version not match to system version.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Please check the device version and system version
2. Please check the release note for the version match

Alarm Probable Cause: The alarm will occur while any sub-alarm occur in the unit.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check the  alarm source to find the root cause
2. Clear the related alarm to clear the overall alarm

Alarm Probable Cause: The alarm will occur while the hardware is fail.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check if any other alarms are occurred
2. Reboot the device
3. Change the device if hardware is fail

Alarm Probable Cause: The alarm will occur while the temperature of RU is higher then threshold.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check if there is FAN alarm(dMRU)
2. Check the environment temperature, need to guarantee the environment temperature no more than 45 degree
3. Check the mechanical of the device, make sure there is no dust in the device
4. Keeps some clearance (20cm) of the device

Alarm Probable Cause: The alarm will occur while DC input power<38V or AC  input power<100V.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Verify input power level
2. Check the connections of the power socket
3. Replace the device

Alarm Probable Cause:
Indicates one of the following SFP problems (aggregated per port):
TX levels(<-3dBm or >5dBm),Temperature,TX disable,
absence (removing the SFP from the cage) and TBD.
Add Port number as Trap parameter.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check if a fiber optic cable is disconnected, make sure the connection is stable
2. Check if SFP problem- try to replace with another SFP
3. Clean fiber optic cable/connector

Alarm Probable Cause:
Indicates one of the following SFP problems (aggregated per port):
RX levels(<-10dBm ),Temperature, RX disable,
absence (removing the SFP from the cage) and TBD.
Add Port number as Trap parameter.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check if a fiber optic cable is disconnected, make sure the connection is stable
2. Check if SFP problem- try to replace with another SFP
3. Clean fiber optic cable/connector

Alarm Probable Cause: Indicates the SFP Synthesizer failure .
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check if a fiber optic cable is disconnected

3. Clean fiber optic cable/connector
4. Check if the pair of SFPs are in same type
5. Check the software version and its release note for the limitation

Alarm Probable Cause: Indicates the temperature of SFP is higher than threshold.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check if the device has the over temperature alarm, and deal with OverallAlarm 
2. Check the SFP install  
3. Change the SFP

Alarm Probable Cause: The alarm will occur while RU is working over its design power consumption.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check the other alarms in the device
2. Check if the output power if it is over the rated level 
3. Check the antenna port connections
4. Reboot the device

Alarm Probable Cause: Indicates the uplink input  of RU is overflow.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Keep the enough distance between UE's and RU's antenna
2. Reboot the device

Alarm Probable Cause: Indicates the downlink input is normal, but the downlink output of device is too low.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check the other alarms in the device
2. Check if the output power is too low 
3. Reboot the device
4. Change the device

Alarm Probable Cause: Indicates the VSWR is more than threshold (2.0).
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check the connection of antenna port, make sure it is connection with antenna or load
2. Check the connected antenna or load is in good condition
3. Change the antenna or load
4. Test the device VSWR 
5. Chane the device

Alarm Probable Cause: Indicates the antenna port VSRW is more than threshold(2.5).
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check the connection of antenna port, make sure it is connection with antenna or load
2. Check the connected antenna or load is in good condition
3. Change the antenna or load
4. Test the device VSWR 
5. Chane the device

Alarm Probable Cause: The alarm will occur while PA is alarm.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check the other alarms in the device
2. Reboot the device
3. Chane the device

Alarm Probable Cause: Module version not match to system version.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Please check the device version and system version
2. Please check the release note for the version match

Alarm Probable Cause: Device service off.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check the other alarms in the device
2. Try clear the related alarm
3. Check the carrier, radio switch config
4. Reboot the device
5. Change the device if hardware issue

Alarm Probable Cause: The RU and EU module type is not match, the RU work abnormal.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check the other alarms in the device
2. Reboot the device
3. Connect the RU to right EU

Alarm Probable Cause: Download Output Power is  less than threshold(-99 dBm as default value)
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check the other alarms in the device
2. Check the input power in the AU
3. Reboot the device
4. Change the device

Alarm Probable Cause: Download Output Power is  more than threshold(48dBm as default value)
Alarm Handle Measures:
1. Check the other alarms in the device
2. Reboot the device
3. Set some ATT value in RU

Alarm Probable Cause: AU system over time delay , the system delays more than 70us.
Alarm Handle Measures:
1.Check the delay threshold no more than 60us
2. Check the fiber link connections
3. Check the total fiber length, which is no more than 10km 
4. Rebootthe device
5. Replace the device

4. Change the AU
